statement.markdown: Implement a function to traverse a singly linked list and return the middle node's value. If the list has an even number of nodes, return the value of the second middle node.
statement.inputFormat: Head of a singly linked list, where each node has a val and next attribute
statement.notes: You may modify the linked list in place

examples.explanation: List has 5 nodes, second middle node is 3

hints.text: Use two pointers to traverse the linked list

editorial.title: Correct Approach
editorial.intuition: Use two pointers, one moving twice as fast as the other. When the fast runner reaches the end of the list, the slow runner will be at the middle.
editorial.optimalSolution: Move two pointers to traverse the linked list
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: When the fast runner reaches the end of the list, the slow runner will be at the middle node.
editorial.dryRun: Start with head and two pointers at head for both fast and slow runners. Move the fast runner two steps at a time and the slow runner one step at a time. Stop when fast runner reaches the end.
